activities_projects.title: Evaluating Democratic Outcomes
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the main outcomes expected from democracy
steps: List the five expected outcomes of democracy from the chapter
Explain each outcome with one real-life example
Write a short paragraph on which outcome is most important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical understanding of democratic outcomes
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of explanation and examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Accountability Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand how democratic governments are held accountable
steps: Research one example where citizens questioned government decisions
Explain how accountability mechanisms were used
Discuss the final outcome
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands practical application of democratic accountability
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of case study analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Democracy vs Dictatorship Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare outcomes under democratic and non-democratic governments
steps: Create a table comparing democracy and dictatorship
Compare them based on decision-making, citizen participation, and transparency
Present the comparison in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns differences between democratic and authoritarian systems

activities_projects.title: Economic Development Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the relationship between democracy and economic growth
steps: Read the section on economic development
Write factors that influence economic growth
Explain why democracy may not always guarantee economic growth
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of economic outcomes of democracy

activities_projects.title: Income Inequality Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Study economic inequality in democratic societies
steps: Identify examples of income inequality in the world
Discuss reasons for inequality
Suggest policies that democratic governments can adopt to reduce inequality

activities_projects.title: Social Diversity Debate
activities_projects.objective: Understand how democracy manages social diversity
steps: Divide the class into groups
Discuss how democracy helps resolve conflicts among different groups
Present arguments with examples
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands conflict resolution in democratic systems

activities_projects.title: Majority Rule Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the concept of majority rule in democracy
steps: Write what majority rule means
Explain why minority rights must be protected
Give two examples from real-life politics

activities_projects.title: Freedom and Dignity Survey
activities_projects.objective: Understand dignity and freedom in democratic societies
steps: Ask five people about the importance of freedom of expression
Record their responses
Summarise the results in a short report
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of report and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: RTI Transparency Activity
steps: Read the RTI example from the chapter
Explain the role of transparency in democracy
Discuss other laws that improve transparency
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands role of transparency and citizen rights

activities_projects.title: Democracy Improvement Proposal
activities_projects.objective: Encourage critical thinking about improving democracy
steps: Identify three weaknesses of democracy
Suggest possible reforms or improvements
Present the ideas as a poster or chart
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking about democratic systems
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and practicality of ideas (10 marks)
